summary:
Claim: Asherman syndrome typically presents with reduced menstrual flow due to uterine scarring impairing endometrial development.  

Evidence: - Uterine adhesions from trauma (e.g., dilation and curettage) lead to thinning of the endometrium, reducing menstrual volume. - While amenorrhea can occur in severe cases, hypomenorrhea is more common and reflects persistent but diminished bleeding due to impaired endometrial regrowth.  

Answer: B

Answer: B. Answer choice: B
